  "network_summary": "42 validators scored. A critical limitation in this dataset is that all validators have null ASN and geolocation data, preventing any meaningful geographic or infrastructure diversity assessment. Consequently, all validators received a neutral/penalized diversity score (40) as their contribution to decentralization is unknown. Software diligence is uniform (all v3.0.0), so no software penalties were applied. Consensus performance varied significantly: 5 validators (v002, v016, v032, v038, v039) achieved perfect or near-perfect 30-day agreement and UNL status, scoring highest (88). Two validators (v006, v010) had near-zero agreement and were scored critically low (10). Two validators (v035, v036) had verified domains but poor consensus (<95%), resulting in moderate-to-low scores despite identity trust. The majority of validators fall in the 80-82 range, reflecting strong consensus but lacking domain verification and geolocation data."
}

}

scores_by_validator_id.network_summary: 42 validators scored. A critical limitation in this dataset is that all validators have null ASN and geolocation data, preventing any meaningful geographic or infrastructure diversity assessment. Consequently, all validators received a neutral/penalized diversity score (40) as their contribution to decentralization is unknown. Software diligence is uniform (all v3.0.0), so no software penalties were applied. Consensus performance varied significantly: 5 validators (v002, v016, v032, v038, v039) achieved perfect or near-perfect 30-day agreement and UNL status, scoring highest (88). Two validators (v006, v010) had near-zero agreement and were scored critically low (10). Two validators (v035, v036) had verified domains but poor consensus (<95%), resulting in moderate-to-low scores despite identity trust. The majority of validators fall in the 80-82 range, reflecting strong consensus but lacking domain verification and geolocation data.
